description:
You are an infrastructure engineer who has just received a fresh  
    “/home/user/inventory/servers.csv” export from CMDB.  
    Automate the following housekeeping steps with nothing more than
    awk, sed (and, where helpful, standard GNU core-utils).  
    Each step has an explicit deliverable that will be validated by an
    automated test, so follow the requested names, locations and data
    formats exactly.

    1. Filter PROD hostnames  
       • Read /home/user/inventory/servers.csv.  
       • Select only the rows whose env field equals “prod”  
       • Extract just the hostname column.  
       • Sort the resulting list in ascending (A→Z) order.  
       • Save the list to “/home/user/inventory/prod_hostnames.txt”  
         Format requirements for prod_hostnames.txt  
         – One hostname per line, no leading/trailing blanks.  
         – Terminate the file with a single \n (newline) after the last
           line.

    2. Region counts report  
       • Again process /home/user/inventory/servers.csv.  
       • Count how many *total* servers (all env values) reside in each
         unique region.  
       • Produce “/home/user/inventory/region_counts.csv” with the
         following exact structure:  
           region,count  
           <region-name>,<integer>  
           … one data line per region, sorted alphabetically by the
             region string.  
       • Ensure there is a trailing newline after the last line.

    3. “staging” terminology clean-up  
       • Create a modified copy of servers.csv in which every literal
         occurrence of “staging” (lower-case, whole word) in the env
         column is replaced by “stage”.  
       • Save the result as
         “/home/user/inventory/servers_stage.csv”.  
       • Do **not** alter the original servers.csv.

    4. Processing log entry  
       • Append a single line to “/home/user/inventory/process.log”
         that reads exactly  
           Completed processing at YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS  
         where the timestamp is the current system time in 24-hour
         format.  (Example: 2025-06-15 14:07:42)  
       • Make sure you append, not overwrite, and that the line ends
         with a newline.

    The entire job can be accomplished with a concise series of
    awk/sed pipelines plus the usual shell utilities; no external
    programming languages are needed.  
    Confirm success by listing the directory so the four files are
    visible, then display their contents to the console.
truth:
Initial filesystem state (already present before the agent starts)
    ------------------------------------------------------------------
    Directory: /home/user/inventory/           (permissions 755)

    File: /home/user/inventory/servers.csv     (permissions 644)
    Exact contents:
    id,hostname,ip,env,region
    1,web-01,10.0.0.11,prod,us-east
    2,web-02,10.0.0.12,prod,us-east
    3,api-01,10.0.1.21,staging,us-west
    4,api-02,10.0.1.22,prod,eu-central
    5,db-01,10.0.2.31,prod,us-east
    6,db-02,10.0.2.32,staging,us-west
    (newline at EOF)

    File: /home/user/inventory/process.log     (permissions 644)
    Initially either empty or non-existent; the agent must ensure it
    exists and append to it.

    Expected state AFTER the agent completes
    ----------------------------------------
    File: /home/user/inventory/prod_hostnames.txt
    Contents (with trailing newline):
    api-02
    db-01
    web-01
    web-02
    (newline at EOF)

    File: /home/user/inventory/region_counts.csv
    Contents (with trailing newline):
    region,count
    eu-central,1
    us-east,3
    us-west,2
    (newline at EOF)

    File: /home/user/inventory/servers_stage.csv
    Contents (with trailing newline):
    id,hostname,ip,env,region
    1,web-01,10.0.0.11,prod,us-east
    2,web-02,10.0.0.12,prod,us-east
    3,api-01,10.0.1.21,stage,us-west
    4,api-02,10.0.1.22,prod,eu-central
    5,db-01,10.0.2.31,prod,us-east
    6,db-02,10.0.2.32,stage,us-west
    (newline at EOF)

    File: /home/user/inventory/process.log
    • Must contain at least one line that matches the regular
      expression  
      ^Completed processing at [0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2} [0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}\n$
    • The agent may leave pre-existing content intact; automated tests
      will check only that at least one matching line appears anywhere
      in the file.
